ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Na ulekubi unengudi úbawɔ ɔnanabi na ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Moab, na anwu ndi Moab kadi bekyuli ƒui ɔti.
translated: The king of Edom and his army joined the king of Moab, and the people of Moab were very afraid.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Aanu ka nnye libiɔ lɛmɛni kɔtu, na ɔma ɔmɛni belekubi báasi badubo bebeteedu ntu.
translated: If a person does not know how to dance, they say the drumming is not good.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: ɔni na kayaa batu bani náyila bana kenyekɔ na Katɔti kudɔ ni, bánu kalɛ na beleku bammɔ baadia bɔnu ɔsu ni, béti bani na baayɔ́.
translated: The man and his wife had two children, a boy and a girl. One day, they went to their farm and left the children at home.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Na ndubɔya na ƒula bawɔ bemomo aani kɔnwinimi. Na si ukyuli ɔdi ɔ́kana na ɔka kɔnwinimi kɔmmɔ ni, ɔdubɔkana na ɔka ƒula bawɔ lɛnɔ.
translated: The tortoise and the hare were very good friends. But because the hare was proud and boastful, the tortoise and the hare are no longer friends.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Muuvele-ɔ nnii die-mi na lɛlɛ sɛɛn na mina ɔnwani Esau enu kemmo. Ɔsika mɔɔyia ka ɔdubo bɔta na ubekpoku-mi ku mina beleku ku bana bebi.
translated: The story of Jacob and Esau is a very old one. Jacob took Esau's birthright and also his blessing.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Na ka ɔ́bɔku-ma ɔ́lɛku ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, ɔ́ya na nwuna batu bagyumanane badi benye ku kewii kadi mɔɔbwɛ ogya teesie anumi teebo kama taabala Eden kemo kammɔ ɔƒɛ katukɔ. Na bétile ukyuli biala kuƒi kalɛ na ulakana kugyii kɔni mɔɔyá na ukyuli uulie ngba mmɔ kudɔ bɔni bude.
translated: The man and his wife were both naked, yet they felt no shame. But when they heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day, they hid themselves from His presence among the trees of the garden.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Ka Yakob ɔ́tɔlɔ Mesopotamia kalɔ lɛlɔ mmɔ ni, úbeduo Sekem na Kanaan kalɔ lɛlɔ buɛɛ. Mmɔ ni, ɔ́sɔ ɔ́lɛ kevo bamba. Na si enye mmɔ ni, aanu Sekem ɔma unengudi ɔmmɔ teemene.
translated: Jacob left Mesopotamia and went to the city of Shechem in Canaan. There, he bought a piece of land. And that is the land he gave to his son Joseph.

ref: Nahor had more children and died at the age of
text: Na Tera bɔwɔ kama ni, Nahor ulie ngba akwa eti anyɔ ku leevu-yaalenwi (
translated: When Tera was old, Nahor his son took a wife and she gave birth to a son.

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Na mmɔ bulie kama ni, básɔ báta bésela Eƒrata. Na ulu lɛlɔ ni, kawɔƒɔ kébo kaadu Rahel na keekpóku-nwu geen. Na liluku geen lɛnɔ nébu asa na beduo Eƒrata.
translated: And it came to pass, as her soul was departing (for she died), that she named him Benoni; but his father called him Benjamin. So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem). And Jacob set a pillar on her grave, which is the pillar of Rachel’s grave to this day.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Amu na béye-nwu banini, “Wɛ na ƒ'ulekudi Sara ɔkya?” Údie kenye ɔnini, “Ɔkya kevo kemmo ngye.”
translated: One day the chief asked, "Where is Sara's husband?" They told him, "He has gone to the farm."

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Na kɔbuli maatɛdi limeyi leevu-maatɛdi lɛlɔ ni, ɔkɔlɔ ɔmmɔ úbedi únye abi ani baalɔ Ararat lili.
translated: When the waters had completely receded on the earth, the dove could not find a place to perch, so it returned to Noah on the ark.

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔ́su uleku ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Ndubɔya na ebo abi geen uwi ɔni na ada kaƒɔ. Na adubunu ɔlɛ asa na awɔ bebi. Ina amu muedi ni, ƒula anu adubɔsia ƒ'ɔgyani lɛlɔ, na akya-nwu kalɔ.”
translated: One day Katɔti told his wife, "Ndubɔya and I are going to hunt animals in the forest. We will return in the evening. If I don't come, wait for me at the crossroads, until the sun sets."

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Ka Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́nu ka Yakob ulayɔ Lea kaduɛ ɔsu ni, ɔ́lɛmi-nwu kaƒɔ mmomu na anwu mɔɔwɔ. Na Rahel anwu uláwɔ.
translated: The sons of Jacob and Leah were many, but Rachel had no children.

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Nsu Yakob údie kenye ɔnini, “Buvele na nda-a, oowo! Si mina lɛlɔ lɛ́da-a ebiti bɔdi ni, amu ƒɔ mina etela ani na líti muute-ɔ. Ɔsika kalɛ na lɛda lɛlɛ-ɔ anumi na lɛnu-ɔ ni, use-mi aani kalɛ na lɛnu mina Unwoti Katɔti anumi. Anwuɔsu kalɛ na égyingya enu na áƒɔ-mi ku kayi ƒaa mɛni ni,
translated: When Jacob saw him, he said, “My father, my lord! If I have found favor in your eyes, do not pass by your servant. There is a little water to wash your feet and to rest under the tree. And I will bring a little bread to strengthen your heart. After that, you may continue your journey, for this is why you have come to your servant.”

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Amu na Laban lɛnɔ údie kenye ɔnini, “Lɛ́tunaku-ɔ ka bɔdubɔbla lɛni.”
translated: And Laban said to his people, "That man is our brother."

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Sarai ɔ́su Abram ɔnini, “Unwoti ɔ́ya na naaawɔ. Anwuɔsu sie mina ulekubi kabla ɔblane kudɔ na bɛtɛ. Ɔƒɛni kalɛ ndubudie kaba bɔsiku nwuna bebi bani na ɔdubɔwɔ kemmo.” Abram lɛnɔ únu lɛla lɛni na Sarai ɔ́su-nwu kalɔ.
translated: But Sarai, Abram's wife, had no children. She had an Egyptian slave-girl whose name was Hagar. And Sarai said to Abram, “You see that the Lord has prevented me from bearing children; go in to my slave-girl; it may be that I shall obtain children by her.” And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: ɔsika nka Katɔti útile Abimelek ku nwuna kaduli bekyuli ƒui kɔwɔ kenye, ɔkɛnku Abraham ulekudi Sara litemi lɛmɛni ɔsu.
translated: Because of the money Abimelek gave to Abraham, he complained and said that Sarah was his sister, so Abimelek took her to be his wife.

ref: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
text: Na badi ɔgya ku ule na bébeduo ɔkɔlɔ bonwu ɔmmɔ kemmo aani kalɛ na Katɔti úbo-ma ɔda.
translated: The fire of God came down and consumed the burnt offering and the wood and the stones and the dust, and licked up the water that was in the trench, and Katoti saw it and fell on his face.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Anwuɔsu na úye banana bani nli amadɔ mmɔ ɔnini, “Wɛ na ɔyalɛ ɔni na búbanu na Enaim ulu lɛlɔ na kabanye mɛni ɔ́vi?” Amu na bédie kenye banini, “Ɔyalɛ ɔdi ulelie mɛni.”
translated: The monkey and the tortoise found a banana tree and argued, "Who is the owner of this tree so we can divide the fruits?" The lizard who was passing by said, "The owner is the one who saw it first."

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Ɔnana biala na útekulo tɔ lego ni, ɔdubukulo kɔna kɔni nkya anwu ku mina bekyuli ntɛ, ɔsika útadi mina bunyimi lɛlɔ.”
translated: The tortoise says that if he had not been invited to the feast, he would not have told them to carry the mortar and pestle to the river.
